refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation—the act of translating mission inputs into performance targets. Recording and managing are secondary concerns that belong to separate atomic FRs. This refinement isolates the core problem: given worksite inputs (mission, constraints, safety plan, operational policies), produce a set of performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability). The FR remains solution-neutral by not prescribing *how* derivation occurs (algorithm, tool, manual process) or *where* records are stored.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that given a documented worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints, equipment class), the system produces a traceable set of performance requirements with clear linkage to source inputs. Acceptance: performance targets are documented and their derivation path is auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements with traceability to worksite inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirement changes in accordance with worksite operational policies.

candidate_dps: DP-NGHE-005.1a: Establish a parameterized mission-context intake process that captures worksite operational profile (duty cycle, environmental conditions, safety constraints) and maps each input to corresponding performance target dimensions (speed, accuracy, reliability, safety margins) via a documented traceability matrix.

DP-NGHE-005.1b: Implement a baseline performance specification repository indexed by worksite class/profile, with versioned snapshots and change-log tracking; derive instance-specific targets by selecting and tailoring the appropriate baseline against current mission inputs.

DP-NGHE-005.1c: Design a real-time performance requirement derivation engine that continuously monitors worksite operational state and dynamically adjusts performance targets within pre-approved safety envelopes, logging all adjustments with timestamp and rationale.

DP-NGHE-005.1d: Establish a manual performance target review and approval workflow triggered by worksite mission changes, with documented sign-off by safety and operations stakeholders; maintain an immutable audit trail of all approved baselines and calibration updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-NGHE-005.1a + DP-NGHE-005.1d (hybrid): Combine a parameterized mission-context intake process (DP-NGHE-005.1a) that systematically captures worksite inputs and maps them to performance dimensions via documented traceability, with a formal approval and audit-trail workflow (DP-NGHE-005.1d) that ensures stakeholder sign-off and maintains immutable baseline history. This hybrid approach balances systematic derivation with governance rigor required by safety-critical context.

potential_sources: Coupling between mission-context intake (DP-NGHE-005.1a) and safety-plan interpretation: Risk that safety constraints are ambiguously translated into performance targets. Mitigation: Define a formal safety-constraint-to-performance-target mapping vocabulary and validate with safety stakeholders during intake design.

Coupling between baseline repository (implicit in DP-NGHE-005.1d) and worksite classification scheme: Risk that baseline selection logic becomes brittle if worksite taxonomy changes. Mitigation: Decouple baseline indexing from worksite class by using feature-based selection (e.g., duty-cycle intensity, environmental hazard level) rather than rigid class names.

Coupling between traceability matrix and performance-target dimensionality: Risk that adding new performance dimensions (e.g., cybersecurity latency) requires rework of intake process. Mitigation: Design intake process to accept extensible performance-dimension schema; version the schema and maintain backward compatibility.
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP avoids tight coupling to specific technologies (e.g., no mandate for particular database, workflow engine, or sensor types). Traceability matrix and audit trail are logical constructs, not implementation specifics. Hybrid approach decouples derivation logic (DP-NGHE-005.1a) from governance/approval (DP-NGHE-005.1d), allowing independent evolution.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is well-defined and stable during a performance-target baseline period. Risk: Rapid mission changes (e.g., emergency operational mode) may invalidate derived targets. Mitigation: Define a change-trigger threshold and escalation path for out-of-envelope mission shifts.

Assumption: Safety plan and operational policies are documented and accessible at intake time. Risk: Policies may be implicit, conflicting, or evolving. Mitigation: Require explicit policy documentation and version control; establish a policy-change notification mechanism.

Assumption: Traceability matrix can be maintained manually or with lightweight tooling. Risk: Matrix becomes stale or inconsistent as worksite conditions and targets evolve. Mitigation: Automate traceability updates where possible; establish a periodic audit (e.g., quarterly) to reconcile matrix against actual worksite state.

Assumption: Stakeholder sign-off (DP-NGHE-005.1d) is feasible within operational timelines. Risk: Approval bottleneck delays deployment or forces shortcuts. Mitigation: Pre-define approval roles, SLAs, and escalation paths; consider tiered approval (e.g., fast-track for minor calibration updates).

Assumption: Baseline history and calibration updates can be stored and retrieved reliably. Risk: Data loss or corruption undermines auditability. Mitigation: Implement immutable audit-log storage (e.g., append-only, cryptographically signed) and regular backup verification.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR-NGHE-005.1 to explicitly distinguish between (1) initial performance-target derivation from worksite mission context and (2) ongoing baseline maintenance and calibration updates. Proposed refined FRs: FR-NGHE-005.1.1: 'Derive initial performance targets from documented worksite mission context, safety plan, and operational policies via a parameterized intake process with full traceability.' FR-NGHE-005.1.2: 'Maintain a versioned, immutable history of performance baselines and calibration updates, with documented rationale and stakeholder approval for each change.' This separation clarifies the boundary between intake logic (DP-NGHE-005.1a) and governance/audit (DP-NGHE-005.1d), reducing ambiguity in acceptance criteria and enabling independent testing of each concern.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Mission planning and requirements management layer that orchestrates parameterized intake processes, traceability mapping, approval workflows, and audit trails for safety-critical mission contexts.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context inputs, worksite parameters, and performance dimension specifications from external task sources or operators.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits approval status, audit trail confirmations, and baseline change notifications to safety-critical oversight systems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges worksite telemetry data for intake mapping and returns traceability/approval status for mission execution feedback.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest cohesion: mission-context intake, parameterized derivation, traceability mapping, approval workflows, and audit trails are all core mission planning responsibilities. Minimal diffusion: only exports approval status and baseline history via SafetyAlert; imports mission context via Tasking. This is the natural home for requirements governance.

Moderate containment: approval and audit-trail aspects align with safety governance, but parameterized intake and traceability mapping are mission-planning concerns, not safety-service concerns. Would require Safety Services to own mission derivation logic, creating tight coupling to mission planning. Diffusion risk: approval logic would leak into mission execution layer.

Poor containment: mission intake, approval workflows, and audit trails are not vehicle-level concerns. Vehicle module should focus on actuation and state management, not mission governance. High diffusion: would scatter mission planning logic across vehicle control layer, violating separation of concerns.

Poor containment: teleoperation is an execution/control interface, not a mission planning layer. Parameterized intake and approval workflows belong upstream of teleoperation. High diffusion: would embed mission governance in operator interface, creating tight coupling and reducing reusability.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (Mission Planning & Requirements Management Team)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The hybrid DP (parameterized intake + approval/audit workflow) is fundamentally a mission-planning and requirements-governance concern. It must systematically capture and validate mission context before execution, maintain immutable baselines, and provide traceability—all upstream responsibilities. NGHE Solution is the only module with sufficient scope and cohesion to own this end-to-end without scattering logic across execution layers. Safety Services can consume approval status and audit trails via SafetyAlert interface, but should not own the derivation or approval logic itself.
containment_risks: Risk: If approval workflow is delegated to Safety Services, mission planning logic becomes fragmented between NGHE Solution and Safety Services, creating bidirectional coupling and complicating traceability audits.

Risk: If intake parameterization is embedded in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, mission context becomes tightly coupled to execution, reducing ability to reuse mission plans across different vehicle types or teleoperation modes.
Risk: Audit trail immutability must be enforced at NGHE Solution level; if audit logic is distributed, baseline integrity cannot be guaranteed.
Risk: External stakeholders (safety reviewers, mission planners) expect a single authoritative source for mission approval and history; scattering across modules would violate this expectation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to DP required. The selected hybrid DP (DP-NGHE-005.1a + DP-NGHE-005.1d) is well-suited to NGHE Solution boundary. However, clarify the interface contract: (1) Tasking interface must include structured mission-context schema (worksite parameters, performance dimensions); (2) SafetyAlert interface must include approval status codes and immutable audit-trail references; (3) Telemetry interface must support bidirectional traceability queries (mission baseline ↔ execution state). This ensures NGHE Solution can fulfill both systematic derivation and governance rigor without diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The hybrid DP (DP-NGHE-005.1a + DP-NGHE-005.1d) directly addresses FR-NGHE-005.1 by combining systematic mission-context intake with formal approval and audit-trail governance. The parameterized intake process ensures worksite inputs are captured and mapped to performance dimensions; the approval/audit workflow ensures stakeholder sign-off and immutable baseline history. Both elements are necessary to satisfy the FR in a safety-critical context. The DP is logically sound and complete.

diffusion_risks: MODERATE RISK: If approval workflow logic is delegated to Safety Services instead of owned by NGHE Solution, mission planning and safety governance become bidirectionally coupled. This would fragment traceability audits and complicate baseline integrity enforcement.

MODERATE RISK: If parameterized intake parameterization is embedded in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, mission context becomes tightly coupled to execution layers, reducing reusability across vehicle types and teleoperation modes.

LOW RISK: Audit-trail immutability enforcement must remain centralized in NGHE Solution; distributed audit logic across modules would compromise baseline integrity guarantees required by safety-critical acceptance criteria.

LOW RISK: External stakeholders (safety reviewers, mission planners) expect a single authoritative source for mission approval and history; scattering across modules violates this expectation and complicates compliance audits.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 0.92 containment score with 0.08 diffusion score—the highest among candidate modules. Mission-context intake, parameterized derivation, traceability mapping, approval workflows, and audit trails are all core mission-planning and requirements-governance responsibilities. The module naturally owns the end-to-end flow from worksite input to baseline approval. Minimal diffusion: only exports approval status and audit-trail confirmations via SafetyAlert interface; imports mission context via Tasking interface. This is the correct architectural home. Safety Services (0.65 containment) would introduce diffusion by owning mission derivation logic; Vehicle (0.15) and Teleoperation (0.2) are execution layers and should not own mission governance.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. The acceptance criteria require: (1) documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs, and (2) history of baselines/calibration updates. NGHE Solution's parameterized intake process directly produces traceable performance targets; the approval/audit-trail workflow maintains immutable baseline history. Both are verifiable through interface inspection (Tasking schema, SafetyAlert audit records) and module-level testing of traceability queries and baseline change logs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: HIGH. The FR is unambiguously allocated to NGHE Solution as the mission-planning and requirements-management layer. The allocation is clear because: (1) NGHE Solution owns the parameterized intake process that captures worksite inputs; (2) NGHE Solution owns the traceability mapping that links inputs to performance dimensions; (3) NGHE Solution owns the approval workflow and audit-trail enforcement that maintains baseline history. No ambiguity exists regarding which module is responsible for deriving performance requirements from mission context. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) are well-defined and support the allocation without overlap.

recommended_revisions: INTERFACE SPECIFICATION: Formalize the Tasking interface contract to include a structured mission-context schema that specifies worksite parameters (e.g., terrain, environmental conditions, operational constraints) and performance dimension specifications (e.g., accuracy, latency, safety margins). This ensures NGHE Solution can systematically map inputs to performance targets.

INTERFACE SPECIFICATION: Formalize the SafetyAlert interface contract to include approval status codes (e.g., APPROVED, PENDING_REVIEW, REJECTED) and immutable audit-trail references (e.g., baseline version IDs, approval timestamps, reviewer identities). This ensures external safety systems can consume governance decisions without duplicating audit logic.

INTERFACE SPECIFICATION: Formalize the Telemetry interface contract to support bidirectional traceability queries: (a) mission baseline → execution state (to verify performance targets are being met), and (b) execution state → mission baseline (to detect deviations and trigger re-derivation if needed). This ensures NGHE Solution can maintain closed-loop traceability during mission execution.

DOCUMENTATION: Add explicit constraint to NGHE Solution module specification: 'Approval workflow and audit-trail logic must remain centralized in NGHE Solution; Safety Services shall consume approval status via SafetyAlert interface but shall not own approval or audit logic.' This prevents diffusion into Safety Services.

DOCUMENTATION: Add explicit constraint to NGHE Solution module specification: 'Parameterized intake process must be decoupled from Vehicle and Teleoperation execution layers; mission context shall be passed via Tasking interface, not embedded in vehicle control or operator interface logic.' This prevents diffusion into execution layers.

TESTING: Define acceptance test for traceability: verify that every performance target in the derived set is traceable back to at least one worksite input via documented mapping. This directly validates the acceptance criterion 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs.'

TESTING: Define acceptance test for baseline history: verify that the audit trail is immutable (no retroactive edits), timestamped, and includes reviewer identity for every baseline change. This directly validates the acceptance criterion 'history of baselines/calibration updates.'

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence. The FR-NGHE-005.1 is sufficiently specific and well-scoped to serve as a leaf-level requirement in the decomposition hierarchy. The hybrid DP (parameterized mission-context intake + formal approval/audit-trail governance) is logically sound, complete, and directly traceable to the FR. The IM allocation to NGHE Solution is architecturally correct with 0.92 containment and minimal diffusion risk. The acceptance criteria are verifiable through interface inspection and module-level testing. The recommended revisions are implementation-level refinements (interface formalization, constraint documentation, acceptance test definition) that do not require further functional decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, the explicit audit-trail and approval-workflow requirements provide sufficient control and traceability without need for sub-level decomposition. Further decomposition would risk over-specification and fragmentation of the mission-governance responsibility.

revise.why: The DP is logically sound but would benefit from explicit formalization of the parameterized intake process and approval workflow as two tightly coupled sub-DPs within the same module boundary. Recommend documenting: (1) DP-NGHE-005.1a: Parameterized mission-context intake process (inputs: worksite parameters via Tasking interface; outputs: structured performance-dimension specifications); (2) DP-NGHE-005.1d: Formal approval and audit-trail governance (inputs: derived performance targets; outputs: approval status and immutable baseline history via SafetyAlert interface). This clarification does not require sub-FR decomposition but strengthens the DP specification for implementation handoff.

assumptions: NGHE Solution module exists and is architecturally positioned as the mission-planning and requirements-governance layer, distinct from execution layers (Vehicle, Teleoperation) and safety-oversight layers (Safety Services).
Tasking interface is available to receive mission context (worksite parameters, operational constraints) from external mission planners or autonomous planning systems.
SafetyAlert interface is available to export approval status and audit-trail confirmations to Safety Services and external safety reviewers.
Telemetry interface supports bidirectional traceability queries to enable closed-loop verification of performance targets during mission execution.

Safety-critical acceptance criteria (documented traceability, immutable baseline history) are enforceable through module-level testing and interface inspection without requiring sub-level decomposition.

The parameterized intake process can be implemented as a configurable, reusable algorithm that maps worksite inputs to performance dimensions without tight coupling to specific vehicle types or teleoperation modes.
open_risks: MODERATE RISK: If Tasking interface schema is not formally specified before implementation, mission-context intake may become ad-hoc and lose systematic traceability. Recommend early formalization of mission-context schema (terrain, environmental conditions, operational constraints, performance dimension specifications).

MODERATE RISK: If approval workflow logic is not explicitly constrained to NGHE Solution ownership, Safety Services may incrementally assume approval responsibilities, fragmenting audit-trail governance. Recommend explicit module-ownership constraint in specification.

MODERATE RISK: If parameterized intake parameterization is not decoupled from Vehicle and Teleoperation execution layers, mission context will become tightly coupled to vehicle control and operator interface logic, reducing reusability. Recommend explicit decoupling constraint in specification.

LOW RISK: Audit-trail immutability enforcement requires careful implementation (e.g., append-only logs, cryptographic hashing, version control). Recommend acceptance test to verify immutability, timestamping, and reviewer identity for every baseline change.

LOW RISK: Closed-loop traceability during mission execution (Telemetry interface bidirectional queries) may introduce latency or performance overhead if not carefully designed. Recommend performance testing of traceability queries under nominal and off-nominal mission conditions.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of Tasking interface contract: mission-context schema (worksite parameters, operational constraints) and performance-dimension specifications (accuracy, latency, safety margins).

Formal specification of SafetyAlert interface contract: approval status codes (APPROVED, PENDING_REVIEW, REJECTED) and immutable audit-trail references (baseline version IDs, approval timestamps, reviewer identities).
Formal specification of Telemetry interface contract: bidirectional traceability query support (mission baseline → execution state; execution state → mission baseline).

Explicit module-ownership constraints in NGHE Solution specification: (1) approval workflow and audit-trail logic must remain centralized; (2) parameterized intake process must be decoupled from execution layers.
Acceptance test for traceability: verify that every performance target in the derived set is traceable back to at least one worksite input via documented mapping.
Acceptance test for baseline history: verify that the audit trail is immutable, timestamped, and includes reviewer identity for every baseline change.
Performance testing of parameterized intake process: verify that mission-context mapping scales across multiple vehicle types and teleoperation modes without tight coupling.

Performance testing of approval workflow and audit-trail enforcement: verify that baseline derivation, approval, and audit-trail recording meet latency and throughput requirements for safety-critical mission planning.
